content.claim: Research documents that deterioration of the public school system led more affluent middle-class parents to lose trust in state institutions and opt for private or denominational schools, with the number of church-run educational institutions doubling since 2010; a 2025 UN assessment warns this private-school growth will leave public schools with fewer resources and higher concentrations of disadvantaged students, increasing segregation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Parental sorting and compositional segregation documented as parallel trends to SEN identification growth, but direct causal link not established
No retrieved source quantifies whether SEN/BTMN reclassification is a cause, consequence, or merely co-occurring with parental switching
School-level data linking SEN classification rates to private-school exit not provided
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
